nodes.name: Webhook
nodes.notes: TEST webhook (separate path). Meta still points at PRODUCTION — do NOT change the Meta webhook URL. To exercise this flow, POST a sample Meta message payload to this test URL, or just use the DEBUG workflow for the integration itself.

nodes.name: IF Real WhatsApp Message
nodes.notes: TRUE = an actual inbound user message (has messages[0].id). FALSE = status callbacks (delivered/read) → just acknowledge 200.

nodes.name: Meta Auto-Reply (YOUR existing node)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER — replace with your EXISTING WhatsApp Cloud API send node (the one that already works). Do not change its credential/token. Branch A must keep working unchanged.

nodes.name: Respond 200 (message)
nodes.notes: Returns 200 to Meta. Keep this fast — Meta retries (and re-sends the same message_id) if it does not get a 200 quickly.

nodes.name: Google Sheets Append (YOUR existing node)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER — replace with your EXISTING Google Sheets append node (unchanged). Branch B continues: Sheets → Build Inbox Payload → Kian Portal Inbox.

nodes.name: Build Inbox Payload
nodes.notes: The robustness fix. Isolates fragile field extraction here so the HTTP node body is trivial and always valid.

nodes.name: Kian Portal Inbox
nodes.notes: WHILE TESTING: onError = stopWorkflow so failures are visible. AFTER confirmed working: change onError to continueRegularOutput so a portal outage never blocks the auto-reply (Branch A is independent anyway).

nodes.name: Inbox Result
nodes.notes: Expect 200 { ok:true, message_inserted:true } first time; { duplicate:true } on a repeated message_id.

nodes.name: Respond 200 (ignored)
nodes.notes: Status callbacks (delivered/read) still need a 200 so Meta stops retrying.
